description: The benchmark trail bike. SWAT-equipped alloy frame and proven geometry that set the standard for trail riding in Australia — versatile, capable, and confidence-inspiring.
The all-rounder trail bike. Trek Alpha Platinum alloy with Fox 34 suspension delivers confident all-day performance from technical climbs to rowdy descents.
Mullet-configured trail weapon. Progressive Norco geometry with a 29/27.5 wheel combo handles anything from flow trails to technical rock gardens with confidence.
Carbon trail excellence. Santa Cruz VPP suspension paired with Fox Factory components makes the Hightower the benchmark high-performance trail bike for serious riders.
Giant Maestro suspension in a capable alloy package. Fox suspension and Shimano Deore drivetrain deliver serious trail performance at a genuinely competitive price point.
Enduro capability without the carbon price tag. 160mm of Fox travel on a proven alloy chassis built for riders who push hard and want the bike to do the same.
The best-value trail bike on the market. RockShox Yari fork and SRAM NX Eagle drivetrain at a price that leaves comparable bikes hard to justify — a genuine steal.
Entry-level trail fun without sacrificing thrills. Full suspension with Marin geometry that builds confidence on descents at a price that makes upgrading from a hardtail an easy call.
Mullet-configured enduro weapon in alloy form. RockShox Lyrik fork and 150mm of quality suspension deliver serious performance at an accessible price point.
XC efficiency distilled. Lightweight alloy hardtail with RockShox SID fork and SRAM Eagle for riders who want maximum pedalling efficiency on cross-country and marathon terrain.
Trek most capable enduro platform. 170mm Fox 38 travel and proven Alpha Platinum alloy frame inspire confidence on the most aggressive terrain you can find.
Direct-to-consumer carbon value. The JEFFSY CF Base uses a carbon frame and capable components to deliver a trail bike that punches well above its price point.
Junior-specific 24 inch hardtail built for the next generation of riders. Shimano gearing and a proper suspension fork properly scaled for riders aged 8 to 12.
The perfect introduction to trail riding for younger riders. A no-nonsense 24 inch hardtail with reliable Shimano components built to grow confidence on the trail.
DJI Avinox flagship eMTB. The 800Wh battery and groundbreaking Avinox M1 motor deliver exceptional range and power in an impressively lightweight carbon chassis.
The most progressive eMTB geometry available, powered by DJI Avinox. Carbon mullet chassis with exceptional range for riders who refuse to compromise on trail feel.
Trek premium enduro eMTB. OCLV Mountain carbon frame with Bosch CX motor and Fox Factory suspension deliver a refined and capable eMTB experience on demanding terrain.
Norco alloy eMTB with 170mm of enduro travel and Shimano EP8 power. Proven geometry and accessible pricing make this the reliable workhorse of the eMTB segment.
Direct-to-consumer eMTB value at its best. Carbon frame, Shimano EP8 motor, and SRAM GX Eagle gearing for serious trail capability at a sharp price point.
The eMTB for riders who dislike eMTBs. The ultra-light Fazua Ride 60 motor adds subtle assistance without sacrificing the natural trail feel of a high-performance mountain bike.
The lightweight eMTB pioneer. Whisper-quiet EP8 RS motor and modular battery in a carbon chassis that rides more like a conventional trail bike than any eMTB should.
French enduro expertise meets DJI Avinox power. Aggressive geometry, massive 800Wh capacity, and proven Commencal build quality for riders who demand maximum range.
The flagship DJI Avinox experience. SRAM XX Eagle Transmission, Fox Factory suspension, and 800Wh in a lightweight carbon chassis — as capable as eMTBs get right now.

Brand new PX platform with the upgraded M2S motor — 150Nm and 1,500W peak in a 2.4kg carbon frame. 40 geometry combinations and default mullet make it the most adjustable eMTB in the range. Released April 2026.
Flagship PX with Fox Factory suspension, SRAM X0 AXS wireless drivetrain and carbon wheels. Same M2S motor and 40-position geometry as the PX Carbon — spec'd to the hilt. Released April 2026.
AMFLOW's range-first platform — removable 800Wh battery you can swap in the field, M2 motor (1,100W peak, 125Nm), carbon frame. Coming to Australia later 2026. Dutch Cargo listing at $6,999 AUD.
Removable 800Wh battery meets M2S power — 150Nm and 1,500W peak with the practicality of swappable batteries. Measured 24.2kg by E-MTB Magazine. Coming to Australia later 2026.
Forbidden most extreme eMTB expression. Race-bred mullet geometry with DJI Avinox power and SRAM X0 Transmission for riders who demand ultimate capability and trail feel.
Specialized benchmark eMTB, now lighter and smarter. Carbon frame with in-house Brose-derived motor, Mission Control app integration, and 840Wh for genuinely all-day riding.
The ultra-light eMTB that rides like a regular bike. The SL 1.2 50Nm motor adds subtle assistance on climbs without the eMTB heaviness that ruins the descent experience.
Giant most aggressive eMTB ready to charge. Carbon frame, Fox Factory suspension, and SyncDrive Pro 3 motor combine for formidable enduro-level riding capability.
Versatile trail eMTB from Giant advanced lineup. Carbon mullet setup with SyncDrive Pro motor balances climbing efficiency with aggressive descending capability all day.
Bosch-powered enduro at a surprisingly sharp price. Proven Bosch CX Gen 5 platform with massive 800Wh capacity for riders who prioritise range and value over weight savings.
Canyon all-mountain eMTB flagship, direct from the factory. Mullet carbon construction and Shimano EP801 power at a price that undercuts most comparable alternatives.
Rocky Mountain proprietary eMTB system in an alloy chassis. The unique Dyname 4.0 motor with Athlete Machine Integration delivers a truly distinct riding experience.
Merida heavyweight enduro eMTB in carbon form. 170mm of travel, Fox Factory suspension, and Shimano EP801 power for riders who demand no compromises on the descents.
Pivot hand-built USA precision in eMTB form. SRAM X0 Transmission and Bosch CX Race motor in a mullet carbon chassis — the pinnacle of American eMTB engineering.

The only serious full-suspension 24 inch kids bike available. Built for 9 to 12 year old shredders who have outgrown beginner bikes and demand real trail performance. 145mm air fork, 120mm rear travel, dropper post, and Maxxis Minion tyres as standard.
Entry-level trail hardtail properly sized for 8–12 year olds. Mechanical disc brakes and an 80mm suspension fork give real trail capability while the 1×6 drivetrain keeps it simple and reliable.
Cube brings proper MTB spec to the 24" category. Hydraulic disc brakes, 60mm fork and 8-speed 1x in a well-proportioned alloy frame — one of the best-equipped bikes at this price point.

The most capable 24" hardtail on this list. 100mm Manitou fork, 11-speed SRAM NX, hydraulic discs, and progressive geometry. Everything a young ripper needs to run trails as fast as their mates on bigger bikes.
Giant's entry 24" trail bike with disc brakes — a meaningful upgrade over V-brake options for wet and technical trails. Simple, durable, and widely available through Giant's AU dealer network.

fork: Giant Crest TR Elite Lite FloTune 140mm
description: Giant designed the fork and shock specifically for kids' weight range (26–46kg). Full suspension with 140mm front / 130mm rear, Maxxis 2.4" tubeless-ready tyres, and a dropper post — the benchmark premium 24" full-sus bike.
Marin's entry 24" trail hardtail. Disc brakes and a proper trail fork — correctly spec'd for the trails without overcomplicating things for younger riders just getting into MTB.
A scaled-down adult trail bike for serious young riders. Air fork at 100mm, Shimano Cues drivetrain, tubeless-compatible tyres. Not a toy — this is a proper technical trail hardtail.

Full-suspension with 130mm front and rear — X-Fusion suspension components, hydraulic disc brakes. For the confident young rider who wants to tackle real enduro terrain. Verify final drivetrain spec with retailer.
Scott's all-round 24" trail hardtail. Hydraulic disc brakes, suspension fork, and at 10.8kg it's one of the lighter bikes in this category. Sold through Bicycle Superstore nationwide.

The most widely available kids trail bike in Australia. Shimano 8-speed, a suspension fork, and Trek's extensive AU dealer network. The reliable entry point — widely stocked, easy to service anywhere.
Kids’ 24" trail hardtail with short-travel coil fork, 1×7 Shimano gearing, alloy wheels with 24×2.35" tyres, and mechanical discs — built for juniors roughly 130–150cm rider height.
